report.summary: Peshawar, Pakistan requires immediate escalation for a CRITICAL flood event affecting 540,000 people. Critical decisions are needed on evacuation routing, hospital continuity, road clearance, and shelter activation.
report.detailed_body: SAR analysis using Sentinel-1 VV/VH ratio confirms widespread flooding across Peshawar, with a mean index of 0.24 and 24.3% damage extent covering 153.37 km². Zones FZ-01 and FZ-02 exhibit critical deep water and high water severity. Flood risk is assessed as CRITICAL with a confidence score of 0.91. Approximately 540,000 people are affected; 14 hospitals including Lady Reading Hospital face high risk, 67 schools are impacted, and 89 km of roads are blocked. Vulnerability score is 8.2 out of 10. The single available evacuation route severely constrains response capacity.
report.technical_analysis: Sentinel-1 SAR was selected because cloud cover is 42%. The analysis uses SAR VV/VH ratio with mean value 0.24 and identifies 22 zones. Damage is estimated at 24.3%, with 89 km of roads blocked and a vulnerability score of 8.2.
recommendations: Prioritize evacuation in critical flood zones.
Deploy emergency medical support near hospitals at risk.
Clear blocked road corridors for rescue access.
Open temporary shelters for displaced families.
Monitor flood expansion using updated satellite imagery.
Evacuate critical flood zones FZ-01 and FZ-02 immediately
Secure access routes to Lady Reading Hospital
Deploy emergency search and rescue teams to isolated zones
Emergency medical supplies and mobile clinics
response_priorities: Activate evacuation support for FZ-01 and FZ-02.
Protect hospital access and stage medical surge teams near high-risk facilities.
Clear blocked road corridors needed for rescue and logistics movement.
assumptions: Satellite, hazard, and impact values are local demo data until upstream agents publish live outputs.
Risk zone geometries are simplified mock polygons for dashboard and report demonstration.
Facility and route coordinates are approximate and intended for prototype visualization.
limitations: No live Band messages are consumed in this local demo run.
No field validation, hydrologic model, or live basemap tiles are used for artifact generation.
Impact estimates should be treated as decision-support indicators, not official counts.

criticality.rationale: Critical flood in Peshawar with 153 km² inundated, 540,000 people at risk, 14 hospitals threatened, and only 1 evacuation route available. Immediate escalation required for life-saving interventions.
trigger_factors: Critical infrastructure at risk (14 hospitals including Lady Reading Hospital)
High-confidence SAR flood extent (153 km², 24.3% damage)
Extensive road blockages (89 km) disrupting access

anomalies.description: Missing pdf_url and map_url in report object
anomalies.recommended_handling: Verify output generation pipeline and re-trigger PDF/Map exports

anomalies.description: Single evacuation route for 540,000 affected people creates extreme operational bottleneck
anomalies.recommended_handling: Validate route count via ground truth or alternative GIS datasets

map_narrative.map_narrative: The static risk map identifies a critical flood event in Peshawar, covering 153.37 km² (24.3% damage extent). High-confidence SAR imagery confirms significant inundation affecting 540,000 people, with critical infrastructure threats and severe logistical bottlenecks due to limited evacuation routes.
key_spatial_findings: 153.37 km² of total inundated area identified via SAR VV/VH ratio
89 km of road networks blocked, severely limiting mobility
High vulnerability score of 8.2 indicating poor local resilience
Single remaining evacuation route creating a critical operational bottleneck
hotspots: Flood Zone FZ-01: Critical inundation and isolation
Flood Zone FZ-02: Critical inundation and isolation
Lady Reading Hospital: High-risk critical facility
map_limitations: SAR-derived data requires field validation for precise damage assessment
Static snapshot does not reflect real-time flood crest movement
Optical validation unavailable due to 42% cloud cover

next_6_hours: Evacuate critical flood zones FZ-01 and FZ-02 immediately
Secure access routes to Lady Reading Hospital
Deploy emergency search and rescue teams to isolated zones
next_24_hours: Clear 89km of blocked roads for logistics flow
Establish temporary shelters for 540,000 affected persons
Deploy medical surge teams to high-risk hospitals
next_72_hours: Conduct field validation of satellite damage assessments
Restore critical utility services to affected school zones
Implement long-term displaced population management plans
resource_priorities: Emergency medical supplies and mobile clinics
coordination_priorities: Manage bottleneck at single available evacuation route
Synchronize satellite data with field reconnaissance teams
Coordinate inter-agency shelter and food distribution

decision_brief.official_summary: Peshawar, Pakistan requires immediate escalation for a CRITICAL flood event affecting 540,000 people. Critical decisions are needed on evacuation routing, hospital continuity, road clearance, and shelter activation.
key_decisions_required: Authorize immediate evacuation support for critical flood zones.
Prioritize hospital access and medical continuity operations.
Assign road clearance resources to rescue and logistics corridors.
